<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The requirement is now clear. The user wants to edit any step in the case workflow at any time, such as correcting a name entered earlier.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Your scenario matches real intake work, and the architecture was partly designed for it — but **editing earlier steps is not implemented yet**. Here is how things work today and what would need to change.

## What you have today

The case detail page is a **single scrollable page with sections**, not a locked linear wizard. All sections (identity, legal residence, documents) are visible at once while the case is in `Intake`:

```49:94:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Pages/RegistrationCaseDetail.razor
        @if (_case.Person is null)
        {
            // ... editable identity form ...
        }
        else
        {
            <AppSection>
                <AppCard Title="Identity" Outlined="true">
                    <MudText Typo="Typo.body1">@_case.Person.GivenName @_case.Person.FamilyName</MudText>
                    // ... read-only display only — no Edit button ...
                </AppCard>
            </AppSection>
        }

        <ResidenceStep Case="_case" CaseId="CaseId" OnUpdated="ReloadCase" />
```

So you can scroll back up to identity while working on residence — but once identity is saved, it becomes **read-only**.

### Step-by-step edit support

| Step | Can edit after saving? | Why |
|------|------------------------|-----|
| **Identity** | No | Domain rejects a second record; `Person` has no update method |
| **Residence category** | No (UI) / Partial (domain) | `SetResidenceCategory` overwrites, but UI hides the form once set |
| **Residence permit** | No (UI) / Yes (backend) | Handler upserts via `ResidencePermit.Update()` |
| **Immigration decision** | No (UI) / Partial (domain) | Domain overwrites; UI is read-only once set |
| **Documents** | Attach only | No remove/replace in current UI |

The identity blocker is the strictest:

```48:56:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCase.cs
    public Person RecordIdentity(IdentityDetails identity)
    {
        EnsureStatus(RegistrationCaseStatus.Intake, nameof(RecordIdentity));

        if (PersonId is not null)
        {
            throw new InvalidRegistrationTransitionException(
                "Identity has already been recorded for this case.");
        }
```

And `Person` only has `Create`, not `Update`.

## What the design intended

[ADR-0004](docs/adr/0004-checklist-over-linear-state-machine.md) chose a **checklist over a linear pipeline** so intake order stays flexible — identity, residence, address, and documents can be filled in whatever order the conversation allows.

[ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) also mentions that data-recording methods should allow **corrections during `Intake` (and later `UnderReview`)**, but that correction path is not built yet.

The design system describes an **edit form pattern** (pre-populated fields, dirty-state save bar, unsaved-changes confirmation) — that is the intended UX for your “go back and fix the name” flow.

## What to build for your scenario

For “recorded identity → started residence → noticed wrong name → fix name”, you would need:

### 1. Domain — `CorrectIdentity` (or `UpdateIdentity`)

Add something like:

```csharp
public void CorrectIdentity(Person person, IdentityDetails identity)
{
    EnsureStatus(RegistrationCaseStatus.Intake, nameof(CorrectIdentity));
    // optionally also allow UnderReview per ARCHITECTURE.md
    if (PersonId is null) throw ...
    person.Update(identity); // new method on Person
    // IdentityEstablished stays true — no checklist change needed
}
```

Keep `RecordIdentity` for the first-time path; use a separate method for corrections so intent is clear in logs/tests.

### 2. Application slice — `CorrectIdentity`

Mirror the existing `RecordIdentity` slice:

- `CorrectIdentityRequest` (same fields)
- `CorrectIdentityHandler` — load case + linked person, call domain, save
- `PATCH /api/registration/cases/{id}/identity` (or `PUT`)
- Validator (same rules as record)

### 3. UI — Edit on the identity card

When `_case.Person is not null`, show read-only summary **plus an Edit button** that:

1. Pre-fills the form from `_case.Person`
2. On save, calls `CorrectIdentityHandler` instead of `RecordIdentityHandler`
3. Reloads the case (same as today)

That matches the design-system edit form template: pre-populated model, save only when dirty.

### 4. Same pattern for other steps

Most of the work is UI + explicit domain methods:

- **Residence category** — domain already allows overwrite; add “Change category” in `ResidenceStep.razor` and re-run policy evaluation
- **Residence permit** — backend already upserts; add edit form pre-filled from `_case.ResidencePermit`
- **Immigration decision** — add `Update` on domain + edit UI
- **Documents** — separate slice (replace/delete) if needed

## Important design choices

1. **When editing is allowed** — Only `Intake`? Also `UnderReview`? Block after `Approved`/`Registered`?
2. **Audit trail** — For a municipal system, consider logging who changed what and when (even a simple `CaseCorrection` event table).
3. **Downstream effects** — Changing nationality might invalidate residence policy; re-run `RegistrationResidenceEvaluator` after identity correction (same as permit updates already do).
4. **Wizard vs. sections** — Phase 4+ may introduce `AppWizard` with a stepper, but corrections still need an Edit action on completed steps; the checklist model supports that without forcing linear navigation.

## Summary

You **cannot** fix a person’s name after identity is recorded today — the UI locks it and the domain throws on a second `RecordIdentity`. The architecture supports flexible, non-linear intake and documents an edit-form pattern, but correction slices and UI are still missing.

The smallest useful first step is **`CorrectIdentity`** (domain + handler + Edit button on the identity card). Residence permit updates are mostly a UI change since the handler already supports upsert.

If you want this implemented, switch to **Agent mode** and I can add the correction flow step by step.

---

_**User**_

Is this something which is planned later on in another phase? 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Checking the roadmap and phase docs for planned correction/edit functionality.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I now have a clear picture and am preparing to summarize.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

**Not as its own phase or slice.** Nothing in [ROADMAP.md](docs/ROADMAP.md) lists “edit/correct intake steps” (e.g. `CorrectIdentity`, change residence category after save). Phases 1–2 only cover **first-time recording**; later phases add new steps (address, household, police, approval), not correction of earlier ones.

It is **implied** in a few places, but not scheduled:

| Where | What it says |
|-------|----------------|
| [ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) | Data-recording methods work during `Intake`, with **“corrections also allowed during UnderReview”** — architectural intent, not implemented |
| [design-system/06-page-templates.md](docs/design-system/06-page-templates.md) | **Edit form** pattern (pre-filled, dirty-state save, unsaved-changes dialog) |
| Phase 3 / Phase 4 docs | **`AppWizard`** (Phase 4+) — stepper navigation; each step saves via its own handler so you can leave mid-wizard without losing data. That helps **navigation**, but does not define correction APIs like `CorrectIdentity` |
| Current code | **Residence permit** already upserts in the handler; identity and most UI sections do not |

So the roadmap assumes flexible, non-linear intake ([ADR-0004](docs/adr/0004-checklist-over-linear-state-machine.md)), and the design system describes how edit forms should look — but **no phase owns “go back and fix the name.”**

**When it would naturally show up:**

1. **Before or with Phase 4** — the wizard template expects revisitable steps; without correction handlers, “back to identity” would still be read-only.
2. **Around Phase 7** — review/approval and “audit trail” make formal corrections more important, but Phase 7 focuses on approve/reject/register, not intake edits.

**Bottom line:** Treat this as a **missing cross-cutting feature**, not something you should wait for a later phase to deliver. A sensible place to add it is a small enhancement now (at least `CorrectIdentity` + edit UI), or explicitly as prep work before Phase 4’s wizard.

If you want it on the roadmap, it could be a short addendum under Phase 2 or a “Phase 2.1 — Intake corrections” slice with `CorrectIdentity`, edit UI for residence/permit, and tests. I can draft that wording if you switch to Agent mode.

---

_**User**_

I mean this is valid for CorrectIdentity but it needs to correct all the other possible steps too. Let's assume we made a mistake in the Legal residence, the user need to be able to correct 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Reviewing every intake step and what correction support exists or is missing for each.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The full scope is now clear. All steps must support correction, not just CorrectIdentity.

A holistic correction approach is needed across all steps. Identity should be read-only. Residence category changes risk leaving stale permits behind. Permit records are read-only after creation.

Immigration decisions overwrite their stored references on each update. Documents are only attached. Address, housing, household, and civil status likely follow the same overwrite pattern. Corrections are permitted during UnderReview across all data-recording methods. The roadmap does not yet include a unified correction feature.

_**Agent (default)**_

You're right to think about this as a **cross-cutting intake correction** concern, not a one-off `CorrectIdentity` slice.

## Is it planned?

**No — not as a unified feature across all steps.** The roadmap adds new recording slices phase by phase (`RecordIdentity`, `SetResidenceCategory`, `DeclareAddress`, …) but never lists matching **correction** slices.

What *is* planned (implicitly):

- **[ADR-0004](docs/adr/0004-checklist-over-linear-state-machine.md)** — intake is non-linear; officers can work in any order.
- **[ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)** — *all* data-recording methods should allow corrections during `Intake` (and `UnderReview`), not only identity.
- **Design system** — edit-form pattern (pre-filled, dirty save, unsaved-changes dialog) and Phase 4+ wizard with revisitable steps.

So the **intent** covers every step; the **roadmap and code** do not implement it consistently yet.

---

## Current state per step (today + future)

| Step | Exists today? | Backend can overwrite? | UI editable after save? |
|------|---------------|------------------------|-------------------------|
| **Identity** | Yes | No — throws if already recorded | No — read-only card |
| **Residence category** | Yes | Yes — `SetResidenceCategory` overwrites | No — form hidden once set |
| **Residence permit** | Yes | Yes — handler upserts via `ResidencePermit.Update()` | No — read-only card |
| **Immigration decision** | Yes | Yes — overwrites reference on case | No — read-only card |
| **Documents** | Yes | Attach only | No remove/replace |
| **Address** | Phase 4 | Not built yet | — |
| **Household / civil status** | Phase 4 | Not built yet | — |
| **Police result** | Phase 6 | Separate aggregate | — |

Legal residence alone is **three sub-steps** (category, permit, decision). Each needs its own correction path and UI.

---

## Why legal residence correction is not trivial

Changing legal residence is not just “show the form again.” Corrections can **invalidate downstream state**:

1. **Category change** (e.g. Non-EU worker → EU citizen) — an existing B card may no longer apply; `LegalResidenceEstablished` should be recomputed (you already have `RegistrationResidenceEvaluator` + `ClearLegalResidenceEstablished()`).
2. **Permit correction** — dates/type may flip the checklist flag; policy must re-run.
3. **Stale combinations** — wrong category + old permit can leave the case in a confusing state until the officer fixes both.

So correction is really: **update data → re-evaluate policy/checklist → show officer what broke**.

That pattern should apply to every step, not only residence.

---

## What a coherent design would look like

Instead of only `CorrectIdentity`, treat intake as **record-or-correct**:

### 1. Domain convention

For each intake field, either:

- **Upsert handlers** — same endpoint/slice for first record and correction (like residence permit already does), or  
- **Explicit `Correct*` methods** — clearer audit intent (`CorrectIdentity`, `CorrectResidenceCategory`, …).

Both work; pick one convention and use it everywhere.

### 2. Shared rules

- Allowed while case is in **`Intake`** (and optionally **`UnderReview`** per architecture).
- Blocked after **`Approved`** / **`Registered`** (or only via formal amendment workflow later).
- After any correction, **recompute checklist flags** (residence policy, future address rules, etc.).
- Optional: append to **`AuditTrail`** (mentioned in DOMAIN.md, not implemented yet — natural fit for Phase 7).

### 3. UI convention (every section card)

Same pattern on identity, legal residence, address, household, documents:

```
[Summary when saved]  [Edit button]
  → opens pre-filled form
  → Save calls correct/upsert handler
  → reload case + refresh checklist chips
```

That matches the design-system **edit form** template and future **AppWizard** (revisitable steps with edit, not locked steps).

### 4. Cascades to define explicitly

| Correction | May also need |
|------------|----------------|
| Identity (name, nationality) | Re-run residence policy; NR duplicate search (Phase 5) |
| Residence category | Warn if permit type mismatches; optionally clear/invalidate permit |
| Permit | Re-run policy only |
| Address (Phase 4) | Clear `AddressConfirmed` if police already ran |
| Document wrong file | Remove/replace document slice |

---

## Where it fits in the roadmap

It is **not** assigned to a phase. Reasonable options:

1. **Phase 2.1 — Intake corrections** (now) — identity + full legal residence + documents; establishes the pattern before Phase 4.
2. **Bake into Phase 4** — build `DeclareAddress` etc. as upsert from day one, and retrofit Phases 1–2.
3. **Phase 7** — formal audit trail for corrections at review time (too late for day-to-day intake fixes).

Option 1 or 2 is the practical choice; waiting until Phase 7 would leave officers stuck with read-only sections for a long time.

---

## Bottom line

Your requirement — **correct any step, including legal residence, after moving on** — matches the architecture and ADR-0004, but **no phase owns it**. Today only residence permit has partial backend support; everything else locks after first save in the UI.

The right scope is a **cross-cutting “intake corrections” capability** for all recording slices (existing and future), with shared rules, re-evaluation, and an Edit action on every section — not a standalone `CorrectIdentity` feature.

If you want this captured in the roadmap, switch to Agent mode and I can add a Phase 2.1 section listing correction slices for identity, residence category, permit, immigration decision, and documents.

---

_**User**_

add this as a phase 2.1 and document well what we need to do

---

Phase **2.1 — Intake corrections** is documented. Here is what was added and updated:

### New document
**[docs/phases/phase-2.1-intake-corrections.md](docs/phases/phase-2.1-intake-corrections.md)** — full phase spec covering:

- **Problem** — per-step gap table (identity blocked in domain; residence/documents blocked in UI)
- **Design decisions** — record-or-correct convention, allowed statuses, checklist re-evaluation, edit-form UI pattern, cascade rules
- **Deliverables checklist** — 14 numbered items (domain, slices, UI, tests)
- **Slice specs** — `CorrectIdentity`, legal residence edits, `RemoveDocument`
- **API routes** — new `PUT identity`, `DELETE document`; existing POST routes documented as upsert
- **UI changes** — `RegistrationCaseDetail`, `ResidenceStep`, document list
- **Demo walkthrough** — name fix, category change + permit update, document remove
- **Tests** — domain + integration scenarios
- **Out of scope** — audit trail (Phase 7), police invalidation (Phase 6), Phase 4+ slices
- **Carries forward** — Phase 4+ must ship correction with first record

### Updated documents
- **[docs/ROADMAP.md](docs/ROADMAP.md)** — Phase 2.1 section between Phase 2 and Phase 3; slice checklist item for correction paths; Phase 4 note to follow the pattern
- **[docs/phases/README.md](docs/phases/README.md)** — Phase 2.1 row in the index
- **[docs/features/registration/README.md](docs/features/registration/README.md)** — intake corrections table
- **[docs/features/README.md](docs/features/README.md)** — Phase 2.1 pointer
- **[docs/phases/phase-2-residence-category-permits.md](docs/phases/phase-2-residence-category-permits.md)** — link to Phase 2.1 as next step
- **[docs/adr/0004-checklist-over-linear-state-machine.md](docs/adr/0004-checklist-over-linear-state-machine.md)** — consequence pointing to Phase 2.1

When you are ready to implement, the phase doc is the implementation checklist. Say if you want to start with a specific slice (e.g. `CorrectIdentity` first).
